bull: Deeply oversold RSI 21 / stochastic 9 after a 15% five-day drop on 6.7x normal volume — classic capitulation/bounce setup
Revenue growth +69% YoY reflects the enlarged post-acquisition scale, and forward PE has compressed to 16.6x vs 47x trailing, pricing in a sharp earnings recovery
bear: The -14% single-day move came directly on the H1 2026 report — earnings down 43% YoY confirms real profitability pressure, not just noise
Price broke below both SMA50 (-9.8%) and SMA200 (-8.4%) — the trend has turned bearish and oversold readings can persist
DSV.CO.summary: DSV crashed 14% in a single day on 6.7x normal volume immediately after its H1 2026 report, with earnings down 43% YoY despite revenue up 69% — a violent post-earnings repricing that has pushed RSI to 21 and stochastic to 9. Forward PE at 16.6x (vs 47x trailing) suggests the market expects a recovery, and the selloff has capitulation hallmarks, but the trend break below SMA50/200 makes this a falling knife until it stabilizes.
DSV.CO.key_risk: Post-earnings gap-downs on freight/logistics names can continue for days as institutions reposition; buying here risks a deeper de-rating if H1 guidance implies structural margin pressure.

bull: Oversold RSI 29 / stochastic 12 near annual support, a potential bounce setup
Forward PE 13x vs 18x sector norm — 28% discount — and a recent analyst fair-value upgrade to DKK 231 vs the current DKK 193 price
bear: -35% from the 52-week high with still-declining 20-day momentum (-12.35%) — the trend is deteriorating, not just noisy
Earnings growth data is unavailable (null), so the fundamental support behind the value thesis can't be confirmed
ROCK-B.CO.summary: Rockwool continues to slide, down 35% from its 52-week high and testing oversold territory (RSI 29, stochastic 12) as building-products demand stays soft. The forward PE discount (13x vs 18x sector) and a recent analyst fair-value upgrade to DKK 231 argue for value, but 20-day momentum is still negative and earnings growth can't currently be confirmed.
ROCK-B.CO.key_risk: Building materials names are cyclical and rate-sensitive; a continued downturn in European construction activity could keep pressuring the stock below its current support zone despite the cheap valuation.

bull: Deep value: forward PE 14.9x vs 25x sector norm — a 41% discount — despite +67% earnings growth YoY
Price remains above both the 50- and 200-day SMA (+5.7%/+6.7%), showing the underlying trend is intact even after a 31% pullback from highs
bear: The newly filed lawsuit against Eli Lilly over GLP-1 advertising signals an intensifying competitive war in obesity drugs that could pressure market share and pricing power
NOVO-B.CO.summary: Novo Nordisk trades at a steep 41% forward PE discount to healthcare peers despite 67% earnings growth, and price action remains above both key moving averages even after a 31% pullback from highs. The newly filed lawsuit against Eli Lilly over GLP-1 advertising underscores how competitive the obesity-drug market has become, which is likely part of why the stock hasn't re-rated back toward its highs.
NOVO-B.CO.key_risk: Continued market-share erosion to Eli Lilly's Zepbound/Mounjaro in the GLP-1 category could keep multiples compressed regardless of near-term earnings strength.

bull: A new government vaccine order (smallpox/mpox) provides revenue visibility, and an active buyback program signals management confidence at these levels
Deeply oversold (RSI 29, stochastic 11) near critical 52-week low support, with forward PE at 18x still a 28% discount to sector
bear: Earnings collapsed -93% YoY and revenue is down -21% — the core business is genuinely deteriorating, not just sentiment-driven
BAVA.CO.summary: Bavarian Nordic is hovering just above its 52-week low with earnings down 93% and revenue down 21% YoY — a genuine fundamental slump, though a fresh government smallpox/mpox vaccine order and an active buyback program offer some support. Technically it's deeply oversold (RSI 29, stochastic 11), which sets up a bounce, but this is a name in earnings distress rather than a clean value story.
BAVA.CO.key_risk: A break below the 52-week low (171.3) would confirm the downtrend and could trigger further technical selling with limited earnings support to cushion the fall.

bull: Strong H1 2026 report — 3.3% revenue growth, resilient private-segment growth, and a solid solvency ratio per the earnings call
High 5.4% dividend yield offers an income cushion
bear: RSI 79.6 / stochastic 97.7 — deeply overbought, stretched short-term with real pullback risk
Earnings are still down 43% YoY, yet the stock trades at a premium (15.3x vs 12x sector) — priced for a recovery not yet visible in the bottom line
TRYG.CO.summary: Tryg has run up to overbought levels (RSI 80, stochastic 98) after a solid H1 2026 report showing 3.3% revenue growth and resilient private-segment performance, but earnings remain down 43% YoY and the stock now trades at a premium to insurance peers. The 5.4% dividend yield is attractive, but the technical setup argues for a near-term pause rather than chasing here.
TRYG.CO.key_risk: Overbought conditions combined with a valuation premium despite weak trailing earnings raise the risk of a short-term pullback, especially given large-claims pressure flagged in the earnings call.

bull: Strong uptrend, +12% over 20 days, trading near its 52-week high with a cheap forward PE (10.15x) and ongoing weekly buybacks
Revenue growth +65% YoY is a strong fundamental tailwind
bear: RSI 69 / stochastic 92 — approaching overbought, momentum could stall near the 52-week high
Earnings are still down -27% YoY despite the strong revenue growth, suggesting margin pressure
ALSYDB.CO.summary: Sydbank is in a strong uptrend, up 12% over 20 days and sitting just 0.4% below its 52-week high, backed by a cheap forward PE (10.15x), 4% dividend yield, and consistent weekly buybacks. Revenue growth of 65% YoY is robust, though earnings are still down 27%, and the stock is now flirting with overbought territory.
ALSYDB.CO.key_risk: Approaching the 52-week high with overbought momentum readings raises the risk of a near-term consolidation even though the underlying valuation remains reasonable.

bull: Strategic Sapporo JV (75% stake, $643M cash proceeds) and a confidential India unit IPO filing (up to $700M) both unlock capital and expand footprint
Steady uptrend (+7% over 20 days) with neutral, non-overbought RSI (55)
bear: Missing revenue/earnings growth data makes it hard to confirm the underlying fundamental trend
Low screener score (19.8) — not standing out on momentum/value signals versus peers
CARL-B.CO.summary: Carlsberg is executing corporate actions that unlock value — a Sapporo joint venture bringing in $643M cash plus a confidential India IPO filing that could raise up to $700M — while the stock trends steadily higher (+7% over 20 days) with healthy, non-overbought momentum. The low screener score reflects unremarkable technical/valuation signals versus the rest of C25 rather than any red flag.
CARL-B.CO.key_risk: Execution risk on the Sapporo JV integration and IPO timing could disappoint if market conditions in Southeast Asia or India shift before completion.

bull: FDA approval for its next-generation inflatable penile prosthesis opens a new growth avenue
Forward PE compressed to 15.6x from 44x trailing, plus a high 5.55% dividend yield, with positive short-term momentum (+8.65% 20d)
bear: Still -36% from its 52-week high and trading below its 200-day SMA (-14%) — the long-term downtrend is not yet confirmed reversed
The large gap between trailing (44x) and forward (15.6x) PE implies a big expected earnings jump that must materialize
COLO-B.CO.summary: Coloplast has stabilized and is recovering (+8.65% over 20 days) with a fresh FDA approval for its next-gen penile prosthesis, and a 5.55% dividend yield plus forward PE compressed to 15.6x offer an attractive entry if the expected earnings recovery plays out. However, the stock remains 36% below its 52-week high and below its 200-day moving average, so the longer-term downtrend isn't confirmed broken yet.
COLO-B.CO.key_risk: The steep gap between trailing (44x) and forward (15.6x) PE bakes in a significant earnings recovery — if that doesn't materialize, the stock could re-rate lower again.

bull: Strong H1 2026 results with net profit of DKK 11.9bn and full-year guidance upgraded to DKK 23-25bn — clear fundamental momentum
Earnings growth +15.2% YoY, revenue +8% YoY, trading near its 52-week high (-2.7%) with healthy, non-overbought RSI (56)
Cheap forward PE (11.3x) with a 4.65% dividend yield
bear: Trading near 52-week highs after a strong run — less margin of safety than names further from their highs
DANSKE.CO.summary: Danske Bank just delivered a strong H1 2026 report — DKK 11.9bn net profit and upgraded full-year guidance to DKK 23-25bn — with earnings up 15% and revenue up 8% YoY, trading near its 52-week high on a cheap 11.3x forward PE and 4.65% yield. This is the strongest fundamental picture among the held positions, with genuine guidance upgrades rather than just technical signals.
DANSKE.CO.key_risk: Having already rallied close to 52-week highs, much of the good news may be priced in, leaving less room for further re-rating unless guidance is beaten again.

bull: Multiple positive catalysts: EU approval for Tepkinly in follicular lymphoma, and a lymphoma combination trial with AbbVie hit its primary endpoint
Strong momentum (+12% 20d, +3.16% today) with revenue growth +25.3% YoY
bear: Earnings down -72.8% YoY despite revenue growth — heavy R&D/commercial investment is compressing profitability
Stochastic 82.5 — approaching overbought after the recent run-up on trial news
GMAB.CO.summary: Genmab is riding a wave of positive newsflow — EU approval for Tepkinly in follicular lymphoma and a lymphoma combination trial with AbbVie hitting its primary endpoint — which has pushed the stock up 12% over 20 days on revenue growth of 25%. Earnings remain deeply negative (-73% YoY) as the company invests heavily in its pipeline, and the stochastic reading (82.5) suggests the rally is getting stretched short-term.
GMAB.CO.key_risk: The stock has already rallied hard on good news; any stumble in upcoming trial readouts or a sell-the-news reaction could trigger a sharp pullback given the stretched short-term technicals.

bull: Steady uptrend (+7.8% 20d) trading near 52-week highs with a cheap forward PE (12.9x) and neutral, healthy RSI (55)
Upcoming Capital Markets Day 2026 could be a catalyst for updated strategy/guidance
bear: Revenue and earnings growth are both essentially flat — limited fundamental momentum behind the price move
ISS.CO.summary: ISS is quietly trending higher (+7.8% over 20 days), trading near 52-week highs on a cheap forward PE of 12.9x, with an upcoming Capital Markets Day that could provide a fresh catalyst. Growth is essentially flat (revenue and earnings both near 0% YoY), so the current momentum is more technical/multiple-driven than earnings-driven, and analyst opinion on valuation remains split.
ISS.CO.key_risk: With flat underlying growth, the stock's re-rating depends heavily on the Capital Markets Day delivering a credible growth story — a disappointing update could stall the recent momentum.

bull: Strong earnings growth +40% YoY on a cheap forward PE (12.7x) and 3.37% dividend yield
Active buyback and share-capital-reduction programs signal capital discipline and shareholder returns
bear: RSI 69.5 / stochastic 81.4 — approaching overbought after the recent run
Still -26% from its 52-week high and below its 200-day SMA — the longer-term trend hasn't fully turned yet
RBREW.CO.summary: Royal Unibrew has bounced 11% over 20 days on strong 40% earnings growth, a cheap 12.7x forward PE, and active buyback/capital-reduction programs, but it remains 26% below its 52-week high and below its 200-day moving average, and short-term momentum indicators (RSI 70, stochastic 81) are now stretched.
RBREW.CO.key_risk: The stock is approaching overbought short-term readings while still working to reclaim its longer-term trend — a stall here would leave it stuck in a range.

bull: Trading above its 200-day SMA (+7%) with steady positive short-term momentum.
bear: RSI near midpoint (48) shows no clear directional edge; screener score (43.4) reflects moderate, non-standout signals.
ORSTED.CO.summary: Ørsted shows modest positive momentum and a reasonable valuation but no strong directional signal.
ORSTED.CO.key_risk: Offshore wind names remain sensitive to interest-rate and subsidy-policy shifts.

bull: Cheap forward PE (9.2x) could attract value buyers if the downtrend stabilizes.
bear: Deep -17.7% below its 200-day SMA with negative short-term momentum across all timeframes signals a persistent downtrend.
NNIT.CO.summary: NNIT remains in a clear technical downtrend, trading well below its 200-day average despite a cheap valuation.
NNIT.CO.key_risk: Continued underperformance versus the sector could keep the stock a value trap without a fundamental catalyst.

bull: Strong uptrend across all timeframes (+7.5% 20d) trading well above both moving averages on a reasonable forward PE (12.9x).
bear: RSI at 68 is approaching overbought, raising the risk of a near-term pullback.
NDA-DK.CO.summary: Nordea is in a solid uptrend with healthy valuation support, though momentum is getting stretched.
NDA-DK.CO.key_risk: Overbought conditions could trigger a short-term consolidation after the recent run.

bull: Positive 20-day momentum (+2.6%) with RSI in a healthy neutral zone.
bear: Still -9.1% below its 200-day SMA, showing the longer-term trend remains soft.
GN.CO.summary: GN Store Nord shows tentative short-term improvement but remains below its longer-term trend.
GN.CO.key_risk: A failure to reclaim the 200-day SMA would confirm the longer-term downtrend is still intact.

bull: Trading above its 200-day SMA (+5.1%) with RSI at 35 suggesting the stock is nearing oversold, a potential bounce zone.
bear: Negative short-term momentum (1d/5d both down) shows near-term selling pressure.
VWS.CO.summary: Vestas is pulling back within a longer-term uptrend, with RSI approaching oversold levels.
VWS.CO.key_risk: Wind/renewables names remain highly sensitive to policy and interest-rate headlines.

bull: Strong uptrend (+18.6% above the 200-day SMA) on a cheap forward PE (11x).
bear: RSI at 81.2 is deeply overbought — high risk of a near-term pullback despite the strong trend.
JYSK.CO.summary: Jyske Bank is in a powerful uptrend and still cheap on forward earnings, but is now technically overextended.
JYSK.CO.key_risk: Deeply overbought RSI (81) raises the odds of a sharp near-term correction.

bull: Well above its 200-day SMA (+22%), confirming a strong underlying uptrend.
bear: 5-day momentum is negative (-2.7%) and RSI has cooled to 40, showing the rally is pausing.
DEMANT.CO.summary: Demant remains in a strong longer-term uptrend that is consolidating over the past week.
DEMANT.CO.key_risk: A deeper pullback from the recent highs could test the 50-day SMA as support.

bull: Very strong 20-day momentum (+17.6%) with RSI still in a healthy, non-overbought range.
bear: Still -12.4% below its 200-day SMA and richly valued (forward PE 22.5x) versus other C25 names.
AMBU-B.CO.summary: Ambu B has staged a sharp rally over the past month but is still working to overcome a longer-term downtrend.
AMBU-B.CO.key_risk: Premium valuation (22.5x forward PE) leaves little room for disappointment if the rally stalls.

bull: Strong single-day bounce (+2.55%) with a reasonable forward PE (14.3x).
bear: Sitting slightly below both the 50- and 200-day SMAs, with negative 20-day momentum (-1.9%).
FLS.CO.summary: FLSmidth is range-bound just below its key moving averages after today's bounce.
FLS.CO.key_risk: Failure to reclaim the 50/200-day SMAs would keep the stock in a neutral-to-bearish technical posture.

bull: Strong uptrend, +24.6% above its 200-day SMA and +16.4% above its 50-day SMA, with RSI still neutral.
bear: 5-day momentum has turned negative (-3.0%), a possible early sign the rally is cooling.
PNDORA.CO.summary: Pandora remains in a robust uptrend on longer timeframes despite a soft week.
PNDORA.CO.key_risk: A break of the 50-day SMA support after such a strong run could trigger profit-taking.

bull: Positive 20-day momentum (+5.6%) trading comfortably above both moving averages.
bear: Low screener score (10.3) reflects unremarkable relative signals versus the rest of C25; forward PE unavailable, clouding valuation.
MAERSK-A.CO.summary: Maersk A shows a steady uptrend but doesn't stand out on the screener's combined signals.
MAERSK-A.CO.key_risk: Container shipping rates remain volatile and could swing sentiment quickly in either direction.

bull: Trading above both moving averages (+7-8%) with RSI in a balanced neutral zone.

NSIS-B.CO.summary: Novonesis B shows a steady but unremarkable uptrend at a premium valuation relative to peers.
